description_full:
Über Uns

Wir sind Proxius – ein dynamisches Start-up mit einer großen Leidenschaft für Process Intelligence! Unsere Mission: Komplexe Prozesse transparent machen, zu automatisieren und mit prozesswissen angereicherte KI zu integrieren um echten Mehrwert schaffen. Wir arbeiten vor allem mit Kunden aus der Telekommunikations- und Energiewirtschaft – deutschlandweit sowie international. Vielleicht kennt man uns außerhalb der Process-Mining-Bubble noch nicht, aber unsere Kunden sind jedem ein Begriff!

Jetzt suchen wir dich, um gemeinsam weiter durchzustarten! 🚀




Deine Mission bei uns:

Gestalte mit uns und unseren Kunden spannende Process-Mining-Projekte.
Entwerfen, Optimieren und Überwachen von Datenmodellen und Datenverbindungen (ETL).
Erstelle Dashboards, Analysen, Automatisierungs-Flows und AI-Solutions, mit dem Ziel nicht nur Transparenz über Prozesse zu schaffen, sondern Potentiale freizusetzen.
Übernehme Verantwortung, werde Spezialist für branchenspezifische Prozesseund leite deine Projekte zum Erfolg
Entwickle coole Standardprodukte auf der Celonis-Plattform, die wir international pitchen und vertreiben.




Das bringst du mit:

Abgeschlossenes Studium in der Wirtschaftsinformatik, Informatik, Mathematik oder einem anderen naturwissenschaftlichen oder betriebswirtschaftlichen Studiengang
Du bringst sehr gute Skills in SQL, BI und Datenbanken mit. Idealerweise bereits in Celonis – mindestens weißt du genau, warum Celonis dein nächster Karriereschritt ist. 
Mindestens 1 Jahr Berufserfahrung im Daten, Prozess und/oder Automatisierungs Space.
Beratungserfahrung ist ein Plus. Wichtig ist, dass du in Kundenprojekten eigenständig agierst und im direkten Austausch professionell, verbindlich und kommunikativ auftrittst.
Fließendes Deutsch und Englisch sind beides ein Muss bei uns. 




Das erwartet dich:

Erfahrene Kollegen, die dir Raum für eigene Ideen geben und dir mit Rat und Tat zur Seite stehen.
Flexibilität, wie und wo du arbeitest – ob remote oder gemeinsam im Büro in München, Berlin oder Köln.
Eine überdurchschnittliche Vergütung (Consultant: 55-65K/Jahr | Senior Consultant: 65-75K/Jahr) und jede Menge spannende Projekte mit echtem Impact!




Warum Proxius?

Weil du bei uns keine Nummer bist, sondern von Anfang an Teil eines motivierten Teams wirst, das Dinge bewegt. Wir leben flache Hierarchien, lieben innovative Technologien und haben richtig Bock, mit dir zu wachsen!




Klingt gut?

Dann bewirb dich jetzt und werde Teil unserer Proxius-Story.

Wir freuen uns auf dich! 💡💪

description_full:
Walaris designs and develops AI-driven, software-defined, and hardware-enabled autonomous systems for defense and security applications. Our software platform uses artificial intelligence, sensor fusion, and edge computing to provide real-time situational awareness for air, land, and sea domains, enabling rapid decisions at machine speed. Our cutting-edge technology is utilized by customers across the defense, national security, government, marine, and critical infrastructure protection markets worldwide. 


At our office in Nuremberg, we are looking for a Procurement and Project Management Office Associate (m/f/d) starting as soon as possible. This will be a part-time (20-30 hours per week) position. 


Responsibilities

Procurement

Execute and optimize the project and operational procurement processes, including requirements planning, inquiries, bid comparison, ordering, delivery tracking, and claims handling
Oversee the selection, evaluation, and development of supplier relationships + manage price and contract negotiations with and prepare confidentiality agreements for suppliers 
Monitor delivery deadlines and quality, initiating corrective actions in case of deviations
Support in inventory planning, minimum stock levels, and disposition + maintenance of item and supplier data in internal systems
Oversee the goods receipt inspection process

Logistics

Organize and plan the shipping of exhibition goods to trade fairs and demonstrations, as well as manage general packaging and shipping activities, such as creation of delivery notes
Prepare and execute international shipments, such as the preparation of ATA Carnets

Order and Project Processing

Gather project status information from internal stakeholders to prepare project reports and track deadlines within projects
Support in the preparation of quotations
Monitor delivery dates with customers as part of order processing


Requirements


Completed commercial training (e.g., industrial clerk, wholesale and foreign trade management clerk) or comparable qualification with several years of experience in procurement or a similar commercial role, ideally in a small or medium-sized enterprise
Good knowledge of procurement, materials management, and basic logistics
Confident handling of quotations, pricing, terms, and delivery conditions
Basic knowledge of contract law and general terms and conditions (GTC) in the context of procurement and supplier contracts
Proficiency in MS Office (especially Excel) and ERP systems
Understanding of cost accounting and economic efficiency (e.g., for evaluating potential savings) is preferred
Experience in the security industry, defense industry, or aviation is preferred
Ability to work independently and take responsibility, including prioritization of tasks and leading negotiations
High sense of responsibility for costs, quality, and the company's delivery capability; willingness to continuously improve processes
Structured, careful, and reliable working style with high accuracy in numbers, deadlines, and contracts
Fluent English skills for communication with international suppliers and internal stakeholders 


Benefits



Join our mission and become part of an innovative, dynamic team! At Walaris, you'll play a vital role in saving lives and safeguarding local and national security by harnessing the power of cutting-edge technologies. This is your chance to break new ground and take on meaningful responsibility as you help develop and deploy industry-defining solutions.

We offer creative freedom, competitive compensation, and a fast-paced, collaborative environment where your ideas and contributions truly matter.

If you're ready to make an impact, we invite you to apply by submitting your cover letter, resume, and transcript through our online application form.

description_full:
Junior Project Manager – Role Description & Qualifications

Role Overview

 The Junior Project Manager supports the successful delivery of projects by assisting with planning, coordination, and progress tracking. This role works closely with senior project leaders and cross-functional teams to ensure project activities remain organized, on schedule, and aligned with defined objectives. The position is ideal for professionals seeking to develop project management skills while gaining hands-on experience in managing tasks, timelines, and resources.

Key Responsibilities

 • Assist in developing project plans, timelines, and task assignments.

 • Coordinate project activities and track progress against milestones.

 • Monitor project schedules and identify potential delays or risks.

 • Support communication between project teams and stakeholders.

 • Prepare project documentation, reports, and status updates.

 • Organize meetings, maintain agendas, and record action items.

 • Help manage project resources and task priorities.

 • Track project issues and support resolution efforts.

 • Ensure project activities follow established procedures and standards.

 • Contribute to continuous improvement of project management processes.

Qualifications and Requirements

 • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Management, Project Management, or a related field.

 • Basic understanding of project management principles and methodologies.

 • Strong organizational and time management skills.

 • Ability to coordinate multiple tasks and priorities simultaneously.

 • High attention to detail and accuracy in documentation.

 • Strong communication and collaboration skills.

 • Familiarity with project management tools or tracking systems is beneficial.

 • Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities.

 • Ability to work effectively within a team-oriented environment.

 • Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and develop project management expertise.

Summary

 The Junior Project Manager plays an important role in supporting project success by helping coordinate activities, track progress, and maintain effective communication among stakeholders. This position offers valuable experience in project management practices and provides a strong foundation for career growth in project leadership roles.

description_full:
Job Description – Junior Project Manager

Purpose of Role

The Junior Project Manager will support project leadership at all stages and collaborate with clients, subcontractors, and team members to ensure successful completion.

The ideal candidate will be IT proficient with a construction background, leadership potential, and the ability to travel across our sites in Europe.

This role offers a unique opportunity to develop before taking the next steps in your career with LPI Group as we continue to grow.


This role requires international travel for up to 75% of working time to support overseas project delivery*


Responsibilities Of Role


Support project team through complex and high-value projects.
Assist with developing project plans, timelines and resource allocations and scheduling. 
Collaborate with clients, contractors, and internal staff to ensure effective communication and successful project delivery.
Provide regular updates to Project Manager and Regional Manager.
Supervise work, ensuring tasks are performed in compliance with company standards, industry regulations, and Health and Safety legislation.
Maintain accurate and up-to-date project documentation, including contracts, change orders, and progress reports.
Support in preparing reports on project status, performance metrics, and financial aspects.


Requirements


1-2 years' experience within the construction or electrical industry.
Knowledge of lightning protection systems, earthing, or surge protection is highly desirable.
Familiarity with technical drawings, specifications, and industry standards.
Exceptional communication skills.
Client relationship experience desirable.
Proficiency in relevant software applications.
Understanding of health and safety regulations related to construction and electrical installations.
Fluency in English; proficiency in additional languages such as German Dutch, French, or Spanish would be advantageous but not required.


About LPI Group

Since our establishment in 2011, Lightning Protection Ireland and Lightning Protection International have delivered cutting-edge lightning protection systems, earthing systems, and surge protection devices across the European data centre, pharmaceutical, commercial, and renewable energy sectors. In January 2021, to celebrate a successful 10 years in business, we launched our rebrand to LPI Group to reflect our growth as ‘Cloud to Ground Protection’ specialists in high-demand industries.

description_full:
Responsibilities
The E-commerce industry has seen tremendous growth in recent years and has become a hotly contested space amongst leading Internet companies, and its future growth cannot be underestimated.

With millions of loyal users globally, we believe TikTok is an ideal platform to deliver a brand new and better E-commerce experience to our users. We are looking for passionate and talented people to join our operations team. Together we can build an E-commerce ecosystem that is innovative, secure and intuitive for our users.

This role is based in our Duesseldorf office. 

Responsibilities
- Be the thought partner of the business leader; Dissemble goals, formulate budget plans based on strategic initiatives, and transform goals into actionable plans;
- Formulate go-to-market, merchant growth and merchandise strategy; evaluate and evolve the strategy to meet business needs;
- Develop acquisition, assortment growth strategy and content growth strategy: define target prioritization, define key metrics and formulate strategy for various subcategories;
- Lead key cross functional initiatives and special projects to deliver growth in selected areas;
- Work with various teams across TikTok Shop to develop plans and deliver business goals: campaign, TikTok Shopping Center, mass acquisition & incubation, etc.;
- Build and monitor the operational index; Facilitate periodical business reviews and reports.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications:
- 5+ years experience in e-commerce operations or e-commerce strategy;
- Analytical, data driven, structured thinking and goal oriented; 
- Ability to articulate thesis and strategies independently;
- Proficiency in English is required to communicate with stakeholder.

Preferred Qualifications
- Understanding of content-based e-commerce is a plus;
- Entrepreneurial and collaborative. Ability to drive successful projects with a wide range of cross-functional teams.
- Quick learner, proactive and resilient. Ability to cope well under pressure and thrive in ambiguous environments.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

description_full:
What you'll actually do

If you're obsessed with high-stakes client relationships, thrive under pressure, and know how to turn complex technical work into outcomes executives actually care about, this is your role.

You will own the client relationship on Swans engagements end-to-end. That means walking into law firms, earning the trust of founders and senior leadership, translating what our Legal Engineers build into undeniable business value, and making sure every engagement drives results that speak for themselves.




At Swans, we are assembling a team of "Avengers": a rare mix of technically fluent operators and elite relationship owners who together transform how US law firms use technology. You are the latter. You are the person in the room when things get tense, when a partner questions the investment, or when the team is resistant to change. You do not flinch. You move things forward.




About Swans

Our Company: We are the operational architect for the fastest growing personal injury firms in the US.

Our Team: We are currently an international team, and we plan to grow our core foundational team deliberately. This role is part of that foundation, contributing to building a robust culture with no ceiling for future opportunities as we scale.

Our Culture: We are energetic, no-BS, intense, results-oriented, and driven to change our industry forever.




Day in the life

The role has a dual nature:

60% client relationship ownership and communication You are the primary point of contact for law firm leadership across active engagements. You run the executive meetings, manage expectations, surface risks early, and make sure the client always knows what was delivered and why it matters.

40% project oversight and cross-functional coordination You work closely with Legal Engineers to understand what is being built, translate deliverables into client-facing language, and ensure every sprint ends with outcomes the client can see and feel.




The client side of the job will have you solve challenges like:

Stakeholder trust: A managing partner is skeptical about the investment after a slow first month. You diagnose the concern, address it directly with data and context, and rebuild confidence without overpromising.

Change management: Staff are resistant to a new system the Legal Engineer has built. You design the adoption approach, run the training sessions, and own the outcome.

Escalation handling: Something breaks, timelines shift, or expectations are misaligned. You are the first call, not the last resort.

Value communication: A firm owner does not see the connection between what was built and what it means for their revenue. You make that connection unmistakably clear.

You will manage anywhere from 2 to 5 active client engagements at a time, owning the relationship layer while being tightly paired with a Legal Engineer who owns the technical layer. Our end-to-end engagements typically run at least 6 months, so you will develop deep knowledge of each client's team, culture, and operational challenges.




Why this changes everything for you

If you are a high-performing account manager or consultant frustrated by bureaucracy, low-value clients, and deals that do not move the needle, this is your off-ramp.

You will be working on transformational projects with law firms doing tens of millions in revenue, sitting across the table from founders and C-suite executives, and owning outcomes that are directly tied to your compensation.

Conversely, if you are an ambitious operator tired of maintaining relationships rather than driving them, this is your place. We do not want account managers who check in. We want people who push, challenge, and deliver.




What we need

Elite executive communication: You have sat across from founders, managing partners, and senior executives and held your own. You know how to deliver difficult news, handle objections without capitulating, and command a room without overpowering it.

Tec

description_full:
Project Manager – M&A Transaction & Post-Merger Integration




Location: Germany or Switzerland (Cologne / Zurich preferred)

Travel: ~30% international

Contract: 12-month fixed-term (with potential for permanent role)

Salary: €80,000–€120,000 + performance-based bonus




The Opportunity

My client is bringing together two powerhouse companies across Europe and Southern Africa to build a next-generation, end-to-end business in the Medical Cannabis sector.




Business 1: (Europe-based) brings strong pharmaceutical development capabilities, regulatory expertise, and an established distribution network. It works closely with physicians and pharmacies and also operates patient-facing clinics, giving it deep access to key European markets and a strong commercial and regulatory footprint.




Business 2: (Southern Africa-based) is a leading EU-GMP certified cultivator and extractor of medical cannabis, with large-scale production capacity, a strong quality focus, and a proven track record of supplying global markets reliably and at scale.




Together, they are merging to form a fully integrated platform that connects every part of the value chain. From cultivation and extraction through to pharma development, regulatory execution, and direct patient access across multiple international markets.




They’re looking for a high-impact Project Manager to sit at the heart of the transaction and integration. You’ll be driving the M&A execution, coordinating stakeholders across multiple countries, and then leading the post-merger integration across operations, finance, regulatory, systems, and commercial workstreams.




It’s a fast-paced, hands-on role with real visibility to senior leadership and investors, and a genuine chance to help build and shape a major new global player in the medical cannabis space from day one.




This role combines:

M&A execution
Program and project management
Operational integration leadership

You will work closely with legal, finance, operations, and regulatory teams across multiple countries.




Key Responsibilities




Phase 1: Transaction Execution (Pre-Close)

Manage the end-to-end transaction plan, including timelines, dependencies, and conditions precedent
Coordinate with legal advisors, financial stakeholders, and investors
Support the cross-border structuring and execution of the transaction
Drive Day-1 readiness planning: governance, reporting, systems, communications
Provide structured reporting to senior leadership




Phase 2: Post-Merger Integration (PMI)

Develop and execute a comprehensive integration roadmap
Translate strategic objectives into clear workstreams with responsibilities and milestones
Coordinate integration across:

Operations & supply chain

Finance & reporting

Regulatory & compliance

Commercial and go-to-market activities

IT systems and data infrastructure

Quality management and governance

Procurement and supplier base

Ensure business continuity, identify risks, and implement mitigation strategies
Track and report synergies and operational improvements
Act as a central interface across international teams, coordinating senior stakeholders
Lead integration governance, progress reviews, and executive reporting




Key Deliverables

Transaction execution plan and tracker
Complete Day-1 readiness plan
Detailed integration roadmap and workstream structure
Ongoing synergy tracking and reporting
Smooth operational continuity during integration
Regular executive-level updates




Candidate Profile

Experience

Background in management consulting, M&A, or complex transformations
Experience with cross-border projects or integrations
Exposure to regulated industries (pharma, healthcare, medical devices, food) advantageous
Comfortable with legal, financial, and investor-facing topics

Skills

Strong project and program management
Ability to structure ambiguity and drive execution
Excellent stakeholder management and communication
Solid commercial and financial acumen

Bist Du ein echter Teamplayer mit einem starken Wertekompass? Bist du ein kluger Kopf mit dem Drang nach kontinuierlicher Weiterentwicklung? Bist du eine proaktive Persönlichkeit mit hands-on Mentalität?




Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig. Komm‘ zu Wavestone und entfalte Dein Potenzial in einem Umfeld, das Dich fördert und unterstützt. Voller Möglichkeiten, gemeinsam die Herausforderungen von heute in die Lösungen von morgen zu verwandeln in der Rolle als Consultant - Project Management & Digital Transformation (all genders) - deutschlandweit - am Standort Deiner Wahl!




Deine Aufgaben




Als Consultant im Fachbereich Project Management Excellence gestaltest Du die digitale Transformation führender Organisationen in Europa. Du arbeitest an Projekten, die Strategie, Organisation und Technologie verbinden – und setzt moderne Methoden sowie AI-gestützte Ansätze ein, um Projekte effizienter und wirkungsvoller zu gestalten.

description_full:
Unsere Mission: Wir machen die Logistik für unsere Kunden einfacher und damit die Kunden im Markt erfolgreicher. Unser Grundsatz: Wir sind ein dynamisches Unternehmen mit einer großen Vergangenheit, einer erfolgreichen Gegenwart und einer starken Zukunft. Unser Versprechen: Dein Einstieg bei BLG LOGISTICS lohnt sich!

Werde Teil unseres Teams IT Operation in Bremen – ab sofort in Vollzeit und mit zwei Tagen vor Ort in Bremen. Als IT-Projekt- und Prozessmanager (m/w/d) mit Schwerpunkt Contract & Automobile Logistics unterstützt du unsere Kunden aus diesen Geschäftsfeldern an verschieden Logistikstandorten dabei, IT-Prozesse zu implementieren und zu optimieren.

Deine Vorteile

Uns gibt es seit 1877, unsere IT-Projekte hingegen sind neu und zukunftsorientiert. Du arbeitest gerne in einem abwechslungsreichen Umfeld in einem Großunternehmen? Neben einem sicheren Arbeitsplatz, einem dynamischen Team und Führungskräften, die dich fördern, bieten wir flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege sowie viel Freiheit zur Gestaltung und Bearbeitung deines Aufgabenbereichs. Erlebe, dass sich dein Einstieg bei uns lohnt!


Durch mobiles Arbeiten sowie Gleitzeit ist die Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Privatleben kein Problem mehr.
Du möchtest dich persönlich und fachlich weiterentwickeln? Wir sind zukunftsorientiert und unterstützen dich dabei.
In der Stadt macht dir Autofahren keinen Spaß? Dann fahre doch vergünstigt mit dem ÖPNV.
Abschalten nach Feierabend? In über 2.000 Premium-Fitnessstudios hast du die Möglichkeit, den Kopf freizubekommen.
Mit dem Rad zur Arbeit? Wir bieten (E-)Bike-Leasing für Mitarbeitende. Geld sparen, fit sein und die Umwelt schützen.
Ob Auszeit oder E-Bike unter dem Weihnachtsbaum – dank Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeld bleiben (fast) keine Wünsche offen.
Per App findest du immer die besten Personalrabatte, z. B. für Urlaub, Mode, Haus & Garten.


Deine Aufgaben


Du begleitest den Start neuer Geschäfte an unseren Standorten bis zum Projektabschluss, dabei bist du im Austausch mit unseren Kunden, IT-Dienstleistern und -Lieferanten sowie beteiligten internen Abteilungen.
Du analysierst die IT-Anforderungen unserer Kunden genau, bewertest und erarbeitest daraus resultierende Lösungen – so steigerst du die Leistungsfähigkeit unserer IT-Prozesse und trägst zur kontinuierlichen Verbesserung bei.
Von der Planung zur Praxis: Du berätst und schulst neue IT-Anwender:innen an Logistiksystemen. Für den praxisnahen Einsatz entwickelst du überzeugende Präsentationen, die du unseren Kunden vorstellst.
Wechselnde Aufgaben im Rahmen der Projektarbeit begrüßt du als willkommene Abwechslung: Du erstellst IT-Kalkulationen sowie Lastenhefte und wirkst an den IT-Pflichtenheften mit.
Auch die Durchführung des Projektcontrollings, Second-Level-Supports, Bearbeitung von Change Requests sowie die Beratung und Betreuung der Anwender:innen liegen in deiner Hand.


Dein Profil

Du willst dich der Herausforderung stellen und zusammen mit einem Team unsere Logistikdienstleistung auf das nächste Level bringen? Du besitzt erste Erfahrungen der Weiterentwicklung und Optimierung von IT-Prozessen mit? Darüber hinaus verfügst du über folgende Fähigkeiten und Qualifikationen:


Ein erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium in Wirtschaftsinformatik, Informatik oder Betriebswirtschaftslehre mit IT-Schwerpunkt und relevante Berufserfahrung.
Alternativ hast du eine IT-nahe Ausbildung wie z. B. Informatikkaufmann:frau oder Fachinformatiker:in absolviert und umfangreiche Berufserfahrung im Projektmanagement und Prozessmanagement gesammelt.
Du denkst anwendungsorientiert, arbeitest eng mit Software-Entwickler:innen zusammen und gibst die Geschäftslogik vor – ob beim Konzipieren einer GUI oder beim Erstellen von Mockups.
Idealerweise verfügst du über IT-Erfahrung in der Kontrakt- oder Automobillogistik und bist erfahren mit relationalen Datenbanken, Qlik Sense oder Power BI zur Analyse von Daten sowie mit EDI-Normen.
Ob Scrum, Wasserfall oder Kanban – bei uns triffst
